rationale: The citing paper references Berger and Milkman (2012) only in grouped citations to support broad background claims about why people share and what content factors can increase word of mouth. The citation is used for general literature positioning in the review and discussion, not for a distinct theoretical mechanism, hypothesis, measure, or empirical design in this study. The core contribution and tests in the citing paper center on referral-source effects and social appropriateness norms, without a specific functional dependence on the cited paper.
